title: Write adversarial smart-contract tests
description: Test not just the happy path but attacker behavior: reentrancy, overflow, access bypass, and malicious external contracts.
body: Steps: 1. Cover the happy path, then write attacker scenarios. 2. Add a malicious contract that re-enters on receiving value and assert it fails. 3. Attempt privileged calls from a non-owner and assert reverts. 4. Fuzz numeric inputs for overflow/underflow and boundary conditions. 5. Add invariant tests (e.g. total supply equals sum of balances) run under random sequences. Rollback point: before changing a value-moving function's logic. Validation: every attack test fails to exploit the contract and invariants hold under fuzzing; happy-path-only tests passing is not evidence of safety. Use a framework like Foundry for fuzzing and invariants.
